key_insight: ProsMAE improves histopathology representation learning by pretraining a masked autoencoder on multi-source whole slide image tiles from diverse cancers and then transferring the frozen encoder for ISUP grade classification.
clinical_relevance: This approach may help build more robust prostate cancer grading models that generalize better across stain, scanner, and tissue variation, supporting computational pathology workflows.
method: Pretrained a MAE encoder on tiles from PANDA, CAMELYON17, and BRACS to capture diverse morphology.
Transferred the encoder to ISUP grade classification via ProsCLS with a frozen backbone and linear head.
Compared against a vanilla MAE frozen linear-probe baseline on a disjoint PANDA split.
evidence_signal: On the evaluated disjoint PANDA split, ProsMAE achieved a higher mean validation quadratic weighted kappa (QWK) than the vanilla MAE baseline.
watch_item: The findings are based on split-specific validation results, so repeated-split testing is still needed to confirm robustness and generalizability.
paper_title: ProsMAE: Multi-Source MAE Pretraining for ISUP Grade Classification
